subject: root mode: ContinuousDeployment on GitFlow topology
notes: All three configs (foundation, strata, git-check) set mode: ContinuousDeployment at root. This is trunk-based semantics applied to a GitFlow branch structure. Branches without explicit mode override inherit ContinuousDeployment, stripping prerelease labels. ADR-001 requires root mode: ContinuousDelivery. Impact: develop, feature, bugfix, hotfix, release branches all produce wrong version format unless they have explicit mode overrides (none do).

subject: master: is-release-branch: true instead of is-main-branch: true
notes: All three configs set is-release-branch: true on master. ADR-001 requires is-main-branch: true, is-release-branch: false. Setting is-release-branch: true activates VersionInBranchName strategy for master, attempting to parse a version from the string 'master' — which contains no version. The interaction with missing source-branches: [] amplifies this into the lineage-traversal bug.

subject: master: source-branches: [] absent
notes: None of the three configs declare source-branches: [] on master. Without it, GitVersion traverses the merge commit parent chain through release back to develop, inheriting develop's alpha label and commit count. This is the proximate cause of the 1.62.0-alpha.261 incident. ADR-001 makes source-branches: [] the canonical declaration of 'primary branch with no inherited lineage'. Affects: prims/.github, foundation, strata, git-check, and likely all other estate repos using this template.

subject: master: mode absent (inherits ContinuousDeployment from root)
notes: Master has no explicit mode: field. With root mode: ContinuousDeployment, master inherits ContinuousDeployment. ADR-001 prescribes master mode: ManualDeployment to produce X.Y.(Z+1)-1+N between tags. With ContinuousDeployment master produces X.Y.(Z+1) (no prerelease) between tags — which is actually closer to the intended behaviour but deceptively so, since the label-leak from missing source-branches corrupts it.

subject: strategies: block absent across all configs
notes: None of the configs declare a strategies: block. GitVersion uses default strategies whose composition has changed across versions. ADR-001 explicitly lists [Fallback, ConfiguredNextVersion, MergeMessage, TaggedCommit, TrackReleaseBranches, VersionInBranchName]. Absence makes configs fragile across GitVersion version upgrades. Risk: estate repos may silently change behaviour on GitVersion upgrades without any config change.

subject: develop: tracks-release-branches absent, mode inherited
notes: Develop has no tracks-release-branches: true and no explicit mode: field. ADR-001 requires tracks-release-branches: true so develop sees release branch versions when determining next version. Develop also inherits root ContinuousDeployment instead of ContinuousDelivery. Combined effect: develop may not correctly track release branch versions, and the prerelease label format may be wrong.

subject: source-branches absent on all branch configs
notes: No branch in any of the three configs declares source-branches. GitVersion uses defaults for lineage traversal. Combined with missing source-branches: [] on master, lineage graphs are entirely implicit and unpredictable. ADR-001 explicitly declares source-branches on every branch. Estate risk: any unusual branch topology (squash merges, orphan branches, rebased branches) may produce unexpected versions with no config-visible explanation.

subject: hotfix: label: beta and broken regex in strata
notes: Strata hotfix has label: beta (static, not {BranchName}) AND regex: ^hotfix/ with no (?<BranchName>.+) capture group. Foundation and git-check have {BranchName} label with capture group — correct per ADR-001 intent. The static beta label in strata means hotfix branches cannot be distinguished by name in prerelease identifiers. The missing capture group would cause {BranchName} substitution to fail if label were changed to {BranchName} without fixing the regex.

subject: hotfix: increment: Patch vs Inherit (all configs)
notes: All three configs set hotfix increment: Patch explicitly. ADR-001 uses Inherit. Explicit Patch prevents +semver: minor on a hotfix from producing a minor bump (the branch increment wins when higher in GitFlow, but Patch is lower than Minor — so +semver: minor WOULD override Patch, unlike +semver: patch not overriding Minor). The practical impact is minor but it restricts flexibility to declare a hotfix that warrants a minor bump.

subject: release: increment: None vs Minor (all configs)
notes: All three configs set release increment: None. ADR-001 uses Minor. Both are defensible: increment: None on a release branch relies entirely on VersionInBranchName strategy (version from branch name), which is correct for release/1.2.0. ADR-001's Minor is the GitFlow builder default and produces correct results when branch name contains version. The None setting is not wrong but it does mean the release branch will ONLY produce the version in the name — no increment-based fallback. Low risk, but should be made explicit.

subject: estate template pattern — single shared config without validation
notes: Foundation, strata, and git-check configs appear to have been copied from a single source template. They share identical defects including the is-release-branch: true on master bug. This indicates the config was written without running the GitVersion v6 validation (or was written for an older GitVersion version). The tooling need: a compliance validator that can be run against any repo's GitVersion.yml to report ADR-001 gap severity. This is the primary motivation for the exemplar-to-tooling pipeline.
